quiz.question: REST resource URLs should prefer…
quiz.explanation: Resources are nouns; HTTP methods express the action.

quiz.question: Idempotent HTTP methods include…
quiz.explanation: Safe/idempotent: GET/HEAD/OPTIONS; idempotent writes: PUT/DELETE. POST is not idempotent.

quiz.question: JWT auth typically sends the token in…
quiz.explanation: Stateless REST: client sends JWT each request; server validates signature/claims.

quiz.question: Best status code for successfully creating a resource?
quiz.explanation: 201 Created (often with Location header) for successful POST create.

quiz.question: Pagination on list endpoints exists mainly to…
options: Bound response size and DB load
quiz.explanation: Never return unbounded lists in production APIs.

quiz.question: Best default for public CRUD HTTP API?
GraphQL caching is harder because…
options: Queries vary by shape; URL cache keys less natural

quiz.question: Mixed architecture pattern?
options: One giant GraphQL for DBs directly

problems.title: Design Order Endpoints
problems.prompt: Sketch REST endpoints for listing a user’s orders (paginated), creating an order, and canceling an order. Include status codes.
problems.solution: GET /users/{id}/orders?page=&size= → 200; POST /orders → 201 + Location; POST /orders/{id}/cancel or PATCH status → 200; missing → 404.

problems.title: Idempotency Key
problems.prompt: How would you make payment creation safe against double-submit from a flaky client?
problems.solution: Require Idempotency-Key. Persist key + response. Retries with same key return the original result without charging twice.

problems.title: Choose API style
problems.prompt: Mobile app needs many screens with different fields from same backend. Options?
problems.solution: GraphQL or a BFF that shapes REST DTOs per screen.

problems.title: Secure GraphQL
problems.prompt: List 3 production controls for a public GraphQL endpoint.
problems.solution: AuthZ per field; max depth/complexity; persisted queries; rate limits; timeout.
